title: Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple
tagline: Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Panniyur Sri Varahamurthy Temple is an ancient Hindu temple complex situated at Kumbidi, Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district of Kerala, India.
overview.description: The Panniyur Sri Varahamurthy Temple is an ancient Hindu temple complex situated at Kumbidi, Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district of Kerala, India. The temple is believed to be the first temple in Kerala consecrated by Parasurama. The temple is dedicated to Varaha, the third avatar of Vishnu, who is positioned with Goddess Bhumi. The temple is included among the 108 Abhimana Kshethrams in Vaishnavate tradition.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kumbidi
topPlaces.description: Kumbidi is a village in Pattambi taluk of Palakkad district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is the administrative headquarters and main commercial centre of Anakkara Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Anakkara (Palakkad)
topPlaces.description: Anakkara is a village and grama panchayat in Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district, Kerala, India, bordering Malappuram district. It is located on the southern bank of Bharathappuzha river (Nila, Ponnani River, or Kuttippuram River).

topPlaces.name: Malamakkavu Ayyappa Temple
topPlaces.description: Malamakkavu Ayyappa Temple is a famous Hindu temple dedicated to lord Ayyappan, located in Anakkara Panchayath in Palakkad district of Kerala. A special flower called "Chengazhinir Poovu" which is traditionally used as offering to deity is found and grown only in the temple pond.

topPlaces.name: Kuttippuram
topPlaces.description: Kuttippuram is a town and a block headquarters, situated in the Tirur Taluk, Malappuram district of Kerala state, India. The town is located 34 kilometres south-west of Malappuram and 8 kilometres west of Valanchery.

topPlaces.name: Kudallur
topPlaces.description: Kudallur is in the Palakkad district of Kerala. It is in Pattambi taluk, bordering Malappuram district, also on the banks of Bharathapuzha.

topPlaces.name: Pattithara
topPlaces.description: Pattithara is a village in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Pandikasala
topPlaces.description: Pandikasala is a small town in Malappuram district in the state of Kerala, India. It is a part of Kuttippuram Gramapanchayat.

topPlaces.name: Irimbiliyam
topPlaces.description: Irimbiliyam is a census town near valanchery in Malappuram district in the state of Kerala, India. There are many small villages like Mankery, Angadi, Thirunilam, Kalarikkal, Shapumpadi, and Palli Padi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (36 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (99 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Panniyur Sri Varahamoorthy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kumbidi, Anakkara (Palakkad), Malamakkavu Ayyappa Temple, Kuttippuram, Kudallur.
